tweets.text:
The viral loop in 4 steps:

1. User gets value
2. User shares (motivation: status/connection)
3. Friend sees + wants same
4. Friend joins

Most apps fail at step 2. They ask for shares without giving a reason.

Western Union: 500,000 agent locations.

Stablecoins: Any smartphone.

The 500K locations were an advantage. Now they're legacy infrastructure.

Digital Euro update:

• Decision: October 2023 (will proceed)
• Preparation phase: 2 years
• Pilot phase: 3 years
• Possible launch: 2028-2030

7+ years from decision to reality.

Yield farming isn't free money.

You're being paid to:
• Provide liquidity
• Take smart contract risk
• Impermanent loss exposure

Understand the trade before taking it.

Web3 onboarding in 2024:

1. Download wallet
2. Write down seed phrase
3. Buy ETH from exchange
4. Wait for KYC
5. Bridge to L2
6. Pay gas fees

7 steps to use an app. This is the UX problem.

El Salvador 3 years later:

• Bitcoin not widely used daily
• Tourism up 30% (crypto tourism)
• IMF still unhappy
• President approval: 90%+

Adoption is complex. Political success ≠ Economic success.

Gas app didn't succeed because of features.

It succeeded because it made teens feel popular without the risk of public rejection.

Psychological safety > Cool features

Argentina: 211% inflation (2023).

Stablecoin adoption among highest globally.

When your currency loses value daily, 'volatile' crypto starts looking stable.

Why TBH spread to 8 states in 9 weeks:

1. Anonymous (no social risk)
2. Positive only (compliments, not bullying)
3. In-app unlock (gamification)
4. School-based (natural network effects)

Design for psychology, not just features.

JAM-DEX (Jamaica CBDC):

• First CBDC in Caribbean
• 200K wallets in first year
• Main use: small merchant payments
• Key insight: started with unbanked, not replacing cash

Smart targeting matters.
